sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me map out the room boundaries by checking where the target gets blocked
# Current: rows 40-44, cols 44-48
# Try LEFT to move back toward corridor
result = action(['LEFT'])
print(f"LEFT: done={result.get('done')}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('O', 'b', 'W'):
        b = n['boundary']
        if len(b) >= 2:
            rows = [p[0] for p in b]
            cols = [p[1] for p in b]
            print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Let me also check the gray room's boundaries
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'g':  # gray
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  g (gray room): rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Try to find the dark gray corridor
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'G':  # dark gray
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  G (dark gray): rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
